text:
A rhyming couplet:
He slammed the door after their morning spat,

A rhyming couplet:
She sulked for hours after the petty spat,

A rhyming couplet:
He could barely remember the foolish spat,

A rhyming couplet:
She regretted every word of the spat,

A rhyming couplet:
He apologised before the end of the spat,

A rhyming couplet:
She forgave him quickly after their silly spat,

A rhyming couplet:
The tracker read the clues within the scat,

A rhyming couplet:
She crouched beside the trail to study the scat,

A rhyming couplet:
The naturalist carefully bagged the scat,

A rhyming couplet:
He photographed the freshly deposited scat,

A rhyming couplet:
She documented every sample of the scat,

A rhyming couplet:
The ranger showed the hikers the fresh scat,

A rhyming couplet:
He identified the species from the scat,

A rhyming couplet:
The raindrop hit the windscreen with a splat,

A rhyming couplet:
She dropped the mango squarely with a splat,

A rhyming couplet:
He stepped into the mud with a loud splat,

A rhyming couplet:
The rotten tomato landed with a splat,

A rhyming couplet:
She smacked the fly upon the wall in a splat,

A rhyming couplet:
He skidded through the puddle with a splat,

A rhyming couplet:
The snowball hit the brickwork with a splat,

A rhyming couplet:
The volcano began to slowly emit,

A rhyming couplet:
She tested what the factory would emit,

A rhyming couplet:
The dockyard crew began the hull refit,

A rhyming couplet:

A rhyming couplet:
He supervised the expensive studio refit,

A rhyming couplet:
The trawler underwent a total refit,

A rhyming couplet:
She managed every stage of the refit,

A rhyming couplet:
He drew the plans for the ambitious refit,

A rhyming couplet:
The jury deliberated to acquit,

A rhyming couplet:
She prayed the evidence would help acquit,

A rhyming couplet:
He hoped the testimony would acquit,

A rhyming couplet:
The defence built a case to acquit,

A rhyming couplet:
She argued passionately to acquit,

A rhyming couplet:
He presented the alibi to acquit,

A rhyming couplet:
She checked the list for anything to omit,

A rhyming couplet:
He reviewed the draft for details to omit,

A rhyming couplet:
She edited the chapter and chose to omit,

A rhyming couplet:
He debated which line he ought to omit,

A rhyming couplet:
She considered every paragraph to omit,

A rhyming couplet:
He struggled to decide what to omit,

The hammock drifted in a gentle sway,

A rhyming couplet:
He watched the tall reeds caught in the sway,

A rhyming couplet:
She danced with the music's natural sway,

A rhyming couplet:
The suspension bridge trembled in a sway,

A rhyming couplet:
She searched for the right words to fully convey,

A rhyming couplet:
He struggled with the meaning to convey,

A rhyming couplet:
She painted what her words could not convey,

A rhyming couplet:
He wrote the letter hoping to convey,

A rhyming couplet:
The old mill showed the signs of long decay,

A rhyming couplet:
She photographed the patterns of decay,

A rhyming couplet:
He documented every stage of the decay,

A rhyming couplet:
The crumbling plaster showed centuries of decay,

A rhyming couplet:
She carried all the cups out on a tray,

A rhyming couplet:
He balanced every glass upon the tray,

A rhyming couplet:
She loaded up the breakfast on the tray,

A rhyming couplet:
He laid the instruments out on the tray,

A rhyming couplet:
She practised all the gestures to portray,

A rhyming couplet:
He researched every detail to portray,

A rhyming couplet:
She studied the character she would portray,

A rhyming couplet:
He rehearsed the dialect he had to portray,

A rhyming couplet:
She watched the candles on the altar alight,

A rhyming couplet:
He kept the lantern blazing and alight,

A rhyming couplet:
She stared at the bonfire burning alight,

A rhyming couplet:
He coaxed the campfire to keep alight,

A rhyming couplet:
She winced at the chilli's ferocious bite,

A rhyming couplet:
He warned the child about the frost's sharp bite,

A rhyming couplet:
She offered the horse an apple for its bite,

A rhyming couplet:
He tasted the sharp citrus for its bite,

A rhyming couplet:
She smiled and carried on with quiet spite,

A rhyming couplet:
He completed the project purely out of spite,

A rhyming couplet:
She published the review entirely out of spite,

A rhyming couplet:
He responded to the insult with cold spite,

A rhyming couplet:
He paced the corridor nervous and uptight,

A rhyming couplet:
She fidgeted in the waiting room uptight,

A rhyming couplet:
He fretted the morning of the exam uptight,

A rhyming couplet:
She woke before the interview uptight,

A rhyming couplet:
The pickpocket relied on his finest sleight,

A rhyming couplet:
She watched the conjurer demonstrate his sleight,

A rhyming couplet:
He practised every manoeuvre with his sleight,

A rhyming couplet:
The magician's hands performed the famous sleight,

A rhyming couplet:
He prepared for one last wild and reckless fling,

A rhyming couplet:
She spun around and launched into a fling,

A rhyming couplet:
He tossed the quoit out for a perfect fling,

A rhyming couplet:
She danced the evening off in a Highland fling,

The citrus dressing added extra zing,

A rhyming couplet:
She squeezed the lemon in to add some zing,

A rhyming couplet:
He grated the zest to give the sauce some zing,

A rhyming couplet:
She crushed the chilli in to give it zing,

A rhyming couplet:
He blended the lime for a refreshing zing,

A rhyming couplet:
She scattered the capers to inject some zing,

A rhyming couplet:
He added the ginger for a peppery zing,

A rhyming couplet:
She drizzled the dressing bright with citrus zing,

A rhyming couplet:
She twisted every corner hard to wring,

A rhyming couplet:
He squeezed the cloth out tight enough to wring,

A rhyming couplet:
She looped the linen ready for the wring,

A rhyming couplet:
He spun the bedsheet out until the wring,

A rhyming couplet:
She pressed the soaking towel through the wring,

A rhyming couplet:
He fed the laundry through the mangle wring,

A rhyming couplet:
She balled the cotton up to fully wring,

A rhyming couplet:
He worked the dishcloth until it wouldn't wring,

A rhyming couplet:
She marched beneath the standard of the rebel,

A rhyming couplet:
He smuggled arms to supply the rebel,

A rhyming couplet:
The captain struggled hard the riot to quell,

A rhyming couplet:
She breathed in deeply trying to quell,

A rhyming couplet:
He reached for something comforting to quell,

A rhyming couplet:
She splashed cold water on her face to quell,

A rhyming couplet:
He took the medication to quell,

A rhyming couplet:
She practised meditation to help quell,

A rhyming couplet:
She treated the fabric hard to repel,

A rhyming couplet:
He coated every surface to repel,

A rhyming couplet:
She sprayed the perimeter hoping to repel,

A rhyming couplet:
He applied the solution to repel,

A rhyming couplet:
She built the barrier attempting to repel,

A rhyming couplet:
He sealed the cracks in the walls to repel,

A rhyming couplet:
She repeated the evidence to dispel,

A rhyming couplet:
He wrote the article hoping to dispel,

A rhyming couplet:
She spoke softly and slowly to dispel,

A rhyming couplet:
He gathered every fact to help dispel,

A rhyming couplet:
She broadcast the findings trying to dispel,

A rhyming couplet:
He shared the science in order to dispel,

A rhyming couplet:
He drove his shoulder into the tight maul,

A rhyming couplet:
She broke away and ran clear of the maul,

A rhyming couplet:
She strained to recall the faded memory,

A rhyming couplet:
He struggled to clearly recall,

A rhyming couplet:
She tried with all her effort to recall,

A rhyming couplet:
He squinted through the haze trying to recall,

A rhyming couplet:
She pressed her temples firmly to recall,

A rhyming couplet:
He searched the faded photograph to recall,

A rhyming couplet:
She prayed that nothing dreadful would befall,

A rhyming couplet:
He worried about the misfortune that might befall,

A rhyming couplet:
She warned of every danger that could befall,

A rhyming couplet:
He dreaded what disaster might befall,

A rhyming couplet:
She listed every hardship that could befall,

A rhyming couplet:
He studied every fate that might befall,

A rhyming couplet:
The captain battened down before the squall,

A rhyming couplet:
She braced the rigging through the sudden squall,

A rhyming couplet:
He reefed the sail ahead of the squall,

A rhyming couplet:
She lashed the cargo down before the squall,

A rhyming couplet:
He watched the black clouds signalling the squall,

A rhyming couplet:
She guided the vessel through the thrashing squall,

A rhyming couplet:
She scattered the almond flake across the top,

A rhyming couplet:
He pressed his nose against the glass to see the flake,

A rhyming couplet:
She vowed she would never forsake,

A rhyming couplet:
He promised he would never forsake,

A rhyming couplet:
She swore upon her honour not to forsake,

A rhyming couplet:
He pledged he would never forsake,

A rhyming couplet:
She wrote the oath she would never forsake,

A rhyming couplet:
He made the promise never to forsake,

A rhyming couplet:
She recognised the gravity of her mistake,

A rhyming couplet:
He owned and apologised for his mistake,

A rhyming couplet:
She documented every detail of the mistake,

A rhyming couplet:
He vowed he would never repeat the mistake,

A rhyming couplet:
She understood the cost of the mistake,

A rhyming couplet:
He explained the origin of the mistake,

A rhyming couplet:
She invited every guest to freely partake,

A rhyming couplet:
He encouraged the travellers to partake,

A rhyming couplet:
She urged the reluctant student to partake,

A rhyming couplet:
He set the table inviting all to partake,

A rhyming couplet:
She prepared the feast for all to partake,

A rhyming couplet:
He gestured at the spread and said partake,

A rhyming couplet:
She poured the crisp and fragrant German hock,

A rhyming couplet:
He selected the vintage from the case of hock,

A rhyming couplet:
She chilled the tulip glasses for the hock,

A rhyming couplet:
He swirled and sipped the Rhineland hock,

A rhyming couplet:
She offered every guest a glass of hock,

A rhyming couplet:
He decanted the aged and elegant hock,

A rhyming couplet:
She pulled the paint-stained linen over as a smock,

A rhyming couplet:
He wore the billowing artist's smock,

A rhyming couplet:
She tied the strings behind the linen smock,

A rhyming couplet:
He rolled the sleeves up of the working smock,

A rhyming couplet:
She embroidered the yoke of the child's smock,

A rhyming couplet:
He laundered the flaxen linen smock,

A rhyming couplet:
She dried the freshly starched and ironed smock,

A rhyming couplet:
She threaded every piece to carefully interlock,

A rhyming couplet:
He fitted every joint to neatly interlock,

A rhyming couplet:
She arranged the puzzle pieces so they'd interlock,

A rhyming couplet:
He designed the system so the parts would interlock,

A rhyming couplet:
She engineered the sections to interlock,

A rhyming couplet:
He ensured each component was made to interlock,

A rhyming couplet:
She wove the strands of colour to interlock,

A rhyming couplet:
He watched the child's lip tremble to a frown,

A rhyming couplet:
She creased her forehead in a thoughtful frown,

A rhyming couplet:
He peered at the invoice with a frown,

A rhyming couplet:
She pressed her lips together in a frown,

A rhyming couplet:
He showed the failed attempt behind his frown,

A rhyming couplet:
She stared at the equation with a frown,

A rhyming couplet:
He counted out the change with a deep frown,

A rhyming couplet:
She earned the title and the great renown,

A rhyming couplet:
He trained for years to finally gain renown,

A rhyming couplet:
She studied hard to build her wide renown,

A rhyming couplet:
He risked his life to grow his renown,

A rhyming couplet:
She dedicated decades to her renown,

A rhyming couplet:
He cultivated carefully his great renown,

A rhyming couplet:
She laboured to establish her renown,

A rhyming couplet:
He published the work that earned him renown,

A rhyming couplet:
She performed the feat that gave her renown,

A rhyming couplet:
He won the prize that spread his renown,

A rhyming couplet:
She authored the text that established her renown,

A rhyming couplet:
He accepted the honour and the renown,

A rhyming couplet:
She smiled humbly despite her wide renown,
